blockingIssues.description: VAL-CROSS-009 assertion 'Errors display toast notification, previous album remains' was not verified. The worker confirmed toast system exists and albums load successfully, but never triggered an actual error scenario. No network failure was simulated, no empty bucket scenario was tested, and no error toast was observed. The implementation is correct (toast.error() in catch block, previous album preserved), but the verification requirement was not met.

appliedUpdates.description: Added status polling intervals documentation (3s for running, 15s for idle) and error handling pattern (toast.error with AbortError handling, previous album preservation) to architecture.md

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit verification methodology for error scenarios: When a feature's expected behavior includes error states (network failure, empty data, invalid input), workers must either (a) simulate the error condition and observe the behavior, or (b) provide code analysis proving the error path is reachable and correctly implemented. Simply confirming 'error handling code exists' is insufficient.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both cross-flow-interactions and fix-error-handling-toast workers verified that error handling code exists (toast.error in catch block) but neither actually triggered an error to verify the behavior. This is a recurring pattern in verification-only features.
